rows.title: Cole and the Hamster Take the Stage
paragraphs: There was going to be a show. Cole had been planning it for days. The stage was the playground. The co-star was the hamster. The crucial prop was a banana phone.
Then everything went wrong. The banana phone broke. Actually broke. In half. Cole froze. The hamster froze. The pillows watched.

Cole took a breath, tumbled forward, and shouted whatever came to mind: "glorp!" The audience leaned in. Sometimes nonsense lands. The light shifted briefly toward midnight blue and then thought better of it. Just then a "zoinks" cracked the silence. Nobody admitted to it. A ghost watched the whole thing happen and offered no comment.

Cole pointed at the broken banana phone and shouted "glorp." The banana phone reassembled itself in roughly the wrong order. The hamster applauded the wrong order. The show was now better. Throughout, Cole stayed confused. Steadily confused. a smoothie sat off to the side, mostly forgotten, definitely still part of the day.
Backstage, Cole high-fived the hamster. The show was unrepeatable, which felt right. Time to pack up and find the next thing.

rows.title: Cole and the Mermaid's Rule

Cole stomped sideways while holding the tiny key. This was a different move than the one the rule said no to. A different move! The rule said nothing back.
Cole held the hot dogs up like a trophy. The mermaid could only watch. "bonk!" yelled Cole. Both things were now true at once. Somebody could have sworn the air went a little midnight blue.
In bed, Cole thought about the loophole and quietly said "squonk" one more time, just to seal it. The eagle said "squonk" too, like a co-signature. Bedtime: earned.

Then the duckling burped. A specific burp. The kind of burp that smells exactly like ice cream. "Oh," said Cole. The duckling tried to look surprised. It was not convincing. Cole slid without thinking. It was a thing they did now. The light shifted briefly toward sunset orange and then thought better of it.

Cole looked straight at the stone giant and said one word: "flumpy." The stone giant confessed immediately. To a different crime. Cole noted it for later. "noodle scarf," went somebody. Nobody asked who.
Back home, Cole high-fived the duckling over the case being officially closed. They both started planning the next caper. Justice, snacks, then more justice.
